summary: Independent QA accepts only the M7 strategic terrain recovery planning package. ADR-011 records the product-owner root-level rejection of the current map drawing and spatial indexing direction, supersedes the polygon-first player-facing terrain/spatial model for content-lock purposes, and defines a bounded DRAFT downstream chain. This acceptance does not approve M7 content lock, does not start downstream implementation, and does not start M7 exit validation.

tests_run.result: Dirty state before QA handoff creation was limited to PROJECT_STATUS.md, project state/message/task files, ADR-011, M7 strategic terrain task files, and message route/outbox files. No apps/**, packages/**, content-source/**, tools/**, CI, gate docs, roadmap, acceptance matrix, manifest, checksums, or validation report paths appeared.

Tracked diff only: PROJECT_STATUS.md, project/goal-mode-state.json, project/messages/outbox/GOAL-MODE-CONTINUATION.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/active/M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-RECOVERY-001.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json.
No output; no dependency-ready task is exposed while M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-RECOVERY-001 remains in REVIEW.

decisions.decision: QA accepts only the recovery planning package for lead integration. This does not approve M7 content lock, does not start M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-SCHEMA-001 or any downstream implementation, and does not start M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001.
The package records the product owner's root-level rejection of both current map drawing and current spatial indexing, not merely a visual polish request.

ADR-011 clearly supersedes the rejected polygon-first player-facing map/spatial model for M7 content-lock recovery and makes district governance footprints overlays rather than hit-test, route-cost, path-preview, or AI-reason authority.

M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-SCHEMA-001, FIXTURE-001, RENDERER-INTERACTION-001, and VALIDATION-001 are DRAFT with sequential dependencies and ready_after_task fields, so they cannot start from this QA acceptance alone.

M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 remains BLOCKED with content_lock_accepted=false and human_gate_state REQUEST_CHANGES_STRATEGIC_TERRAIN_MAP_AND_SPATIAL_INDEX_REQUIRED. M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 remains DRAFT with no branch/worktree.

ADR-011 and downstream tasks require negative coverage for hidden grid/lattice/cell leakage, visual-neighbor-without-route, visually-distant-explicit-route, barrier-without-crossing, governance-adjacent-but-route-blocked, geometry perturbation without route change, and renderer-only fake routes.

The package preserves historical/cultural overclaim boundaries and does not authorize high-fidelity final art, formal GIS, production cultural assets, server/multiplayer, telemetry/accounts/cloud save, Rust/WASM, release/commercial/branding decisions, or manual node battle reversal.

risks.summary: The accepted plan will require later changes across schema, fixture/content, renderer/interaction, AI/player explanation, save/query boundaries, and validation.
risks.mitigation: Keep the downstream chain sequential and DRAFT until each task receives its own start, implementation, review, and validation.

risks.summary: Validation includes screenshot judgment that the map no longer reads as a disguised cell board.
risks.mitigation: Pair screenshot review with concrete query parity, route-preview, hit-test priority, anti-grid/lattice/cell, and renderer-only fake route tests.

risks.summary: Low-fidelity terrain, port, river, pass, route, polity, or label content can become false historical assertion if not tagged and reviewed.
risks.mitigation: Fixture work requires historical_researcher review and HISTORICAL/INFERRED/COMPOSITE/FICTIONAL tagging before formal content claims.

requested_action: Lead may integrate the accepted recovery planning package only. Keep M7 content lock unapproved, keep downstream strategic terrain implementation tasks unstarted until separately routed, and keep M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 disabled until strategic terrain validation enters main and product owner explicitly reconsiders content lock.
